---
# TestPlan — Cuppanudel New Feature Work
# Generated by: Testing Agent (05-ta-test-suite)
# Task ID: 05-ta-test-suite
# Phase: 1 (TestPlan generation — no code written yet)

---

## Executive Summary

This TestPlan defines integration tests for all 24 ComponentDefinition deliverables. 
The focus is on cross-component contracts — behavior that spans at least two modules.
Single-component unit tests are the responsibility of the Implementation Agent.

HTML-only changes (`index-html-*` components) are listed in coverage_gaps with rationale 
"HTML-only change; verified by visual inspection and browser test" since they have no 
JavaScript integration test surface.

**Total Test Cases**: 37 integration tests + 1 unit test (test-runner self-check)

---

## Artifact

```yaml
component_ids:
  - test-runner
  - config/property-mapper
  - timing/tempo-context-additions
  - config/sample-provider-registry
  - audio/builtin-click-provider
  - timing/metronome-refactor
  - config/workspace
  - ui/context-menu
  - ui/edit-config-modal
  - config/content-service
  - audio/local-file-provider
  - audio/recordings-provider
  - pool/media-pool-minor
  - audio/media-pool-sample-provider
  - ui/sample-set-picker
  - config/preset-store
  - ui/preset-bank
  - visualizers/alignment-monitor

test_cases:

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-INFRA: test-runner
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-001"
    type: unit
    component_id: test-runner
    description: "test-runner self-check: the test harness can register and execute tests"
    inputs: "Registration of synchronous and asynchronous test cases with assert helpers"
    expected_output: "run() logs PASS/FAIL lines and returns { passed, failed, total } with correct counts"
    determinism_note: "No external I/O; tests are pure in-memory assertions"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-A: config/property-mapper
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-002"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/property-mapper
    description: "property-mapper enforces two-pass atomic write (no partial writes on error)"
    inputs: |
      Schema: [{key:'a',type:'int'},{key:'b',type:'int',required:true}]
      Source: {a:1}
      Target: {a:100, b:200}
    expected_output: |
      Returns error array ['b: required field missing']
      Target object is NOT modified (remains {a:100,b:200})
    determinism_note: "Pure function; no randomness or time dependency"

  - id: "tc-003"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/property-mapper
    description: "property-mapper clamps out-of-range scalars with warning, not error"
    inputs: |
      Schema: [{key:'bpm',type:'int',min:20,max:300}]
      Source: {bpm:400}
      Target: {}
    expected_output: |
      Writes target.bpm=300
      Returns warning string ['bpm: 400 out of range 20–300, clamped to 300']
    determinism_note: "Deterministic clamping logic"

  - id: "tc-004"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/property-mapper
    description: "property-mapper validates array length via exactLength reference"
    inputs: |
      Schema: [
        {key:'bpm',type:'int'},
        {key:'offsets',type:'float[]',exactLength:'bpm'}
      ]
      Source: {bpm:3, offsets:[0, 0.5]}
      Target: {}
    expected_output: |
      Returns error ['offsets: length 2 does not match bpm (3)']
      Target is NOT modified
    determinism_note: "Deterministic length check"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-B (pt 1): timing/tempo-context-additions
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-005"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/tempo-context-additions
    description: "TempoContext carries new clickProviderRef field with correct default"
    inputs: "Call createTempoContext()"
    expected_output: |
      Returned object has clickProviderRef === 'built-in:default'
      Returned object has snapThreshold === 0
      Returned object has beatAccents array (length 4, first=true, rest=false)
    determinism_note: "Deterministic field initialization"

  - id: "tc-006"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/tempo-context-additions
    description: "setBeatsPerMeasure resets beatAccents but preserves clickProviderRef/snapThreshold"
    inputs: |
      tc = createTempoContext(); 
      tc.clickProviderRef = 'sample-set:custom'; 
      tc.snapThreshold = 0.01
      setBeatsPerMeasure(tc, 5)
    expected_output: |
      tc.beatAccents === [true, false, false, false, false]
      tc.clickProviderRef === 'sample-set:custom' (unchanged)
      tc.snapThreshold === 0.01 (unchanged)
    determinism_note: "Deterministic field update"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-B (pt 2): audio/builtin-click-provider
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-007"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/builtin-click-provider
    description: "BuiltinClickProvider returns null before init() resolves"
    inputs: |
      provider = builtinClickProvider (freshly imported, not yet initialized)
      Call getSample(0) before init()
    expected_output: |
      getSample(0) returns null
      getSample(1) returns null
      A console.error is logged with text 'BuiltinClickProvider not initialised'
    determinism_note: "Mocked console.error; no Web Audio I/O"

  - id: "tc-008"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/builtin-click-provider
    description: "BuiltinClickProvider.init() synthesizes AudioBuffers and caches them"
    inputs: |
      provider = builtinClickProvider
      Create mock AudioContext via OfflineAudioContext
      Call await provider.init(mockCtx)
      Call getSample(0) and getSample(1) after init()
    expected_output: |
      getSample(0) returns an AudioBuffer with duration ~0.07s
      getSample(1) returns a different AudioBuffer with duration ~0.07s
      getSample(2) returns null (out of range)
      provider.count() returns 2
    determinism_note: "Mocked Web Audio via OfflineAudioContext (deterministic)"

  - id: "tc-009"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/builtin-click-provider
    description: "BuiltinClickProvider.init() called twice is a no-op (no re-synthesis)"
    inputs: |
      provider = builtinClickProvider
      mockCtx = OfflineAudioContext
      Call await provider.init(mockCtx)
      buf1 = getSample(0)
      Call await provider.init(mockCtx) again
      buf2 = getSample(0)
    expected_output: |
      buf1 === buf2 (same buffer instance)
      Second init() returns immediately (no re-synthesis)
    determinism_note: "Buffer instance identity check"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-B (pt 3): config/sample-provider-registry
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-010"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/sample-provider-registry
    description: "Registry auto-registers BuiltinClickProvider at import time"
    inputs: "Import config/sample-provider-registry module"
    expected_output: |
      registry.get('built-in:default') returns a SampleProvider object
      registry.list() includes a provider with id 'built-in:default'
      provider.getSample(0) returns null (not yet init'd via main.js)
    determinism_note: "Module-level setup; no Web Audio I/O"

  - id: "tc-011"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/sample-provider-registry
    description: "Registry.register() throws on duplicate id; _reset() clears all"
    inputs: |
      Create mock SampleProvider { id: 'test:provider', label: '...', ... }
      Call registry.register(provider)
      Try to register same id again
      Call registry._reset()
    expected_output: |
      Second register() throws TypeError with text 'id already registered'
      After _reset(), list() returns []
      After _reset(), get('built-in:default') returns undefined (not re-registered)
    determinism_note: "Deterministic state management; no I/O"

  - id: "tc-012"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/sample-provider-registry
    description: "Registry.list() returns a snapshot array (mutation does not affect state)"
    inputs: |
      Create and register mock provider
      arr1 = registry.list()
      arr1.splice(0, 1) (mutate returned array)
      arr2 = registry.list()
    expected_output: |
      arr2.length === 1 (full list, unaffected by arr1 mutation)
      arr1 !== arr2 (different array instances)
    determinism_note: "Array reference semantics"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-B (pt 4): timing/metronome-refactor
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-013"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome.start() calls clickProvider.getSample() to schedule AudioBufferSourceNodes"
    inputs: |
      tc = createTempoContext() with beatAccents=[true,false]
      mockProvider = { getSample: (idx) => idx===1 ? mockBuf1 : mockBuf0, count: ()=>2, init: async()=>{}, ... }
      mockCtx = OfflineAudioContext
      metro = createMetronome(mockCtx, tc, mockProvider)
      Call metro.start()
    expected_output: |
      For beat 0: getSample(1) is called (beatAccents[0]=true → accent index)
      For beat 1: getSample(0) is called (beatAccents[1]=false → non-accent index)
      AudioBufferSourceNodes are scheduled via mockCtx.createBufferSource()
      No oscillator or noise synthesis occurs
    determinism_note: "Mocked Web Audio context; deterministic call sequence"

  - id: "tc-014"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome respects beatVolumes threshold; volume < 0.01 silences beat"
    inputs: |
      tc = createTempoContext() with beatVolumes=[1.0, 0.0, 0.005]
      mockProvider returns valid buffers
      metro = createMetronome(mockCtx, tc, mockProvider)
      metro.start()
    expected_output: |
      Beat 0 (volume 1.0): AudioBufferSourceNode created
      Beat 1 (volume 0.0): No AudioBufferSourceNode created
      Beat 2 (volume 0.005): No AudioBufferSourceNode created (below 0.01 threshold)
    determinism_note: "Deterministic volume threshold check"

  - id: "tc-015"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome logs console.error when getSample() returns null"
    inputs: |
      mockProvider.getSample() always returns null
      mockCtx = OfflineAudioContext
      metro = createMetronome(mockCtx, tc, mockProvider)
      metro.start()
    expected_output: |
      console.error is called with text containing 'metronome: getSample() returned null'
      No AudioBufferSourceNode is created
      No exception is thrown
    determinism_note: "Mocked console.error; deterministic null handling"

  - id: "tc-016"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome.restart() stops, resets to beat 0, and resumes with current tc values"
    inputs: |
      metro.start(); 
      Advance time so beat position is non-zero
      metro.restart()
    expected_output: |
      getPlayheadPosition() immediately returns 0.0 after restart
      Metronome resumes scheduling from beat 0
      Any BPM changes in tc since start() are used by the restarted scheduler
    determinism_note: "Mocked time advancement in OfflineAudioContext"

  - id: "tc-017"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome.getPlayheadPosition() derives from measureStart/nextBeatTime, not playbackStart"
    inputs: |
      metro.start() at time T0
      Advance AudioContext.currentTime to T1
      Measure playhead position P1
      Update tc.bpm
      Measure playhead position P2 at same relative time
    expected_output: |
      P1 and P2 are equal (playhead position invariant across BPM change)
      Playhead is NOT derived from playbackStart (which would drift on BPM change)
    determinism_note: "Mocked time in OfflineAudioContext"

  - id: "tc-018"
    type: integration
    component_id: timing/metronome-refactor
    description: "Metronome throws TypeError if clickProvider is not provided"
    inputs: "Call createMetronome(ctx, tc, undefined)"
    expected_output: "TypeError with message 'createMetronome: clickProvider is required'"
    determinism_note: "Deterministic type check"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-C (pt 1): config/workspace + property-mapper integration
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-019"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/workspace
    description: "importWorkspace orchestrates validation and component importConfig in dependency order"
    inputs: |
      workspaceYAML = "version: 1
global: { bpm: 120 }
metronome: { ... }
sampleSets: []"
      components = { global, metronome, sampleSets, presets }
      Mock each component.importConfig() to track call order
      Call importWorkspace(yaml, 'config.yaml', fileSize, components)
    expected_output: |
      Components' importConfig() called in order: sampleSets → global → metronome → presets
      Each importConfig receives its corresponding slice
      Errors from any importConfig prevent further execution
    determinism_note: "Deterministic call sequence; mocked component APIs"

  - id: "tc-020"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/workspace
    description: "importWorkspace enforces 1 MB file size cap and shows error toast"
    inputs: |
      yaml = 'x' repeated 1_048_577 times
      filename = 'config.yaml'
      fileSize = 1_048_577
      Call importWorkspace(yaml, filename, fileSize, components)
    expected_output: |
      Returns false without parsing
      Error toast is shown with text 'File too large for a workspace config'
      No component.importConfig() is called
    determinism_note: "Deterministic size check; mocked toast"

  - id: "tc-021"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/workspace
    description: "importWorkspace parses JSON when filename ends in .json, else YAML"
    inputs: |
      yaml = "{ \"global\": { \"bpm\": 120 } }"
      Call importWorkspace(yaml, 'config.json', size, components)
      Call importWorkspace(yaml, 'config.yaml', size, components)
    expected_output: |
      .json file: parsed via JSON.parse()
      .yaml file: parsed via jsyaml.load(yaml, {schema: jsyaml.CORE_SCHEMA})
    determinism_note: "Deterministic parser selection"

  - id: "tc-022"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/workspace
    description: "exportWorkspace calls each component.exportConfig() and assembles YAML"
    inputs: |
      components = { 
        global: { exportConfig: () => {bpm:120} },
        metronome: { exportConfig: () => {...} },
        ...
      }
      Call exportWorkspace(components)
    expected_output: |
      Returns YAML string with keys: version, global, metronome, sampleSets, presets
      Each section contains the result of the component's exportConfig()
    determinism_note: "Deterministic assembly; no I/O"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-C (pt 2): ui/context-menu
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-023"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/context-menu
    description: "Context menu shows 'Paste Config' hidden (not greyed) when clipboard unavailable"
    inputs: |
      Mock navigator.clipboard.readText to reject with NotAllowedError
      Create context menu with a target element
      Long-press (600ms) on element
    expected_output: |
      Context menu appears
      'Paste Config' menu item is hidden (display:none or visibility:hidden)
      Item is not greyed out (opacity remains 1.0)
    determinism_note: "Mocked clipboard API; deterministic long-press timing (mocked)"

  - id: "tc-024"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/context-menu
    description: "Context menu calls component.importConfig() when 'Paste Config' is clicked"
    inputs: |
      workspaceYAML in clipboard = valid YAML
      Click 'Paste Config'
    expected_output: |
      navigator.clipboard.readText() is called
      YAML is parsed with jsyaml.load(text, {schema: jsyaml.CORE_SCHEMA})
      property-mapper.validateAndApply is invoked per spec
      Menu closes
    determinism_note: "Mocked clipboard; deterministic parse path"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-C (pt 3): ui/edit-config-modal
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-025"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/edit-config-modal
    description: "Edit config modal enforces singleton (only one instance visible at a time)"
    inputs: |
      Create two edit-config-modal instances
      Call modal1.show()
      Call modal2.show()
    expected_output: |
      modal2 is visible, modal1 is hidden
      Reopening modal1 does not break modal2
    determinism_note: "Deterministic DOM state"

  - id: "tc-026"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/edit-config-modal
    description: "Edit config modal parses Apply text with CORE_SCHEMA and validates via property-mapper"
    inputs: |
      User enters YAML text in modal
      Click 'Apply'
      YAML has an invalid field value
    expected_output: |
      jsyaml.load(text, {schema: jsyaml.CORE_SCHEMA}) is called
      validateAndApply is invoked
      If errors, modal shows error panel and remains open
      If no errors, modal closes and onApply callback is invoked
    determinism_note: "Deterministic parse; mocked property-mapper"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-D (pt 1): config/content-service
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-027"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/content-service
    description: "ContentService is empty at import and accumulates registrations"
    inputs: |
      Import config/content-service
      Call contentService.providers
    expected_output: |
      providers is empty array []
    determinism_note: "No auto-registration; deterministic initial state"

  - id: "tc-028"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/content-service
    description: "ContentService.register() throws on duplicate id; unregister() is idempotent"
    inputs: |
      Create mock ContentProvider { id: 'local-files', label: '...', ... }
      Call contentService.register(provider)
      Try to register same id again
      Call contentService.unregister('nonexistent')
    expected_output: |
      Second register() throws TypeError with text 'id already registered'
      unregister('nonexistent') does not throw
      After unregister('local-files'), providers is empty
    determinism_note: "Deterministic registry semantics"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-D (pt 2): audio/local-file-provider and audio/recordings-provider
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-029"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/local-file-provider
    description: "LocalFileProvider.browse() returns [] on user cancel (AbortError)"
    inputs: |
      Mock showOpenFilePicker to reject with DOMException(name='AbortError')
      Call localFileProvider.browse()
    expected_output: |
      Promise resolves (does not reject) with empty array []
      No error toast is shown
    determinism_note: "Mocked File Picker API"

  - id: "tc-030"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/recordings-provider
    description: "RecordingsProvider normalizes undefined (from pool) to null for missing clips"
    inputs: |
      Create RecordingsProvider with mock PoolRef
      pool.getBuffer() returns undefined for a clip id
      Call provider.getSample(index) for that slot
    expected_output: |
      getSample returns null (not undefined)
      console.warn is logged indicating buffer not found
    determinism_note: "Deterministic normalization"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-D (pt 3): pool/media-pool-minor
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-031"
    type: integration
    component_id: pool/media-pool-minor
    description: "Media pool documents that getBuffer() returns undefined (not null) on miss"
    inputs: |
      Create media pool
      Call pool.getBuffer('nonexistent-id')
    expected_output: |
      Returns undefined (not null)
      Callers (RecordingsProvider, MediaPoolSampleProvider) normalize to null
    determinism_note: "Deterministic return value"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-E (pt 1): audio/media-pool-sample-provider
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-032"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/media-pool-sample-provider
    description: "MediaPoolSampleProvider.getSample() normalizes pool undefined to null"
    inputs: |
      Create MediaPoolSampleProvider with slots [{index:0,clipId:'abc'}]
      pool.getBuffer('abc') returns undefined
      Call getSample(0)
    expected_output: |
      getSample returns null (not undefined)
      console.warn is logged
    determinism_note: "Deterministic normalization"

  - id: "tc-033"
    type: integration
    component_id: audio/media-pool-sample-provider
    description: "MediaPoolSampleProvider.init() resolves immediately (no async work)"
    inputs: |
      provider = createMediaPoolSampleProvider(...)
      Call await provider.init(mockCtx)
    expected_output: |
      Promise resolves synchronously
      Buffers are already in the pool (no decoding)
    determinism_note: "Synchronous promise resolution"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-E (pt 2): ui/sample-set-picker
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-034"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/sample-set-picker
    description: "SampleSetPicker constructs MediaPoolSampleProvider and calls onProviderChange"
    inputs: |
      Create picker with registry, pool, tc, metronome
      User taps slot 0 → selects clip 'clip-id-1'
      User taps slot 1 → selects clip 'clip-id-2'
      User taps 'Confirm'
    expected_output: |
      onProviderChange callback is invoked with a SampleProvider instance
      Provider.getSample(0) returns clip 'clip-id-1'
      Provider.getSample(1) returns clip 'clip-id-2'
      Picker calls registry.register(provider)
      Updates tc.clickProviderRef = provider.id
    determinism_note: "Deterministic UI state flow"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-F (pt 1): config/preset-store
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-035"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/preset-store
    description: "PresetStore persists to localStorage and loads on creation"
    inputs: |
      Create store1, save({bpm:120,...}) to slot 0
      Destroy store1
      Create store2 (should load from localStorage)
      Call store2.load()
    expected_output: |
      store2.load()[0] contains the saved snapshot (bpm:120)
      localStorage key 'cuppanudel.presets.v1' contains JSON array
    determinism_note: "Mocked localStorage; deterministic JSON serialization"

  - id: "tc-036"
    type: integration
    component_id: config/preset-store
    description: "PresetStore.importConfig() validates and applies workspace import"
    inputs: |
      store = createPresetStore()
      Call store.importConfig({presets: [snapshot0, snapshot1, null, ..., null]})
    expected_output: |
      store.load() returns the imported array
      Workspace import overwrites all slots atomically
      Errors in any slot are reported, no partial writes
    determinism_note: "Deterministic validation and atomic write"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-F (pt 2): ui/preset-bank
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-037"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/preset-bank
    description: "PresetBank.applyPreset writes snapshot fields to tc and calls metronome.restart() if running"
    inputs: |
      Create preset bank with store, tc, metronome
      store.load()[0] = {bpm:140, beatsPerMeasure:3, ...}
      metronome.isRunning() = true
      Click slot 0 (non-save mode)
    expected_output: |
      tc.bpm is updated to 140
      tc.beatsPerMeasure is updated to 3
      tc.clickProviderRef is updated from snapshot
      metronome.restart() is called
    determinism_note: "Deterministic field update sequence"

  - id: "tc-038"
    type: integration
    component_id: ui/preset-bank
    description: "PresetBank does NOT call metronome.restart() if metronome is stopped"
    inputs: |
      metronome.isRunning() = false
      Click slot 0 to apply preset
    expected_output: |
      tc fields are updated
      metronome.restart() is NOT called
      metronome remains stopped
    determinism_note: "Deterministic conditional logic"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-G: visualizers/alignment-monitor
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-039"
    type: integration
    component_id: visualizers/alignment-monitor
    description: "AlignmentMonitor derives scroll from measureStart/nextBeatTime, not getPlayheadPosition()"
    inputs: |
      Create monitor with mock analyserNode, canvas, tc, metronome
      Call monitor.draw() at times T0 and T1
      Change tc.bpm between draws
    expected_output: |
      Column advancement is proportional to (measureStart advance / measureDuration)
      Advancement is invariant across BPM changes (no drift)
      getPlayheadPosition() is NOT called
    determinism_note: "Mocked analyserNode; deterministic draw algorithm"

  - id: "tc-040"
    type: integration
    component_id: visualizers/alignment-monitor
    description: "AlignmentMonitor does not allocate Float32Array in RAF loop"
    inputs: |
      Create monitor
      Call draw() in a loop 100 times
      Monitor internal allocation count
    expected_output: |
      No new Float32Array instances allocated during draw()
      Array is pre-allocated at construction
    determinism_note: "Memory leak test; no I/O"

  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # LANE-WIRE: poc/main.js-refactor
  # ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  - id: "tc-041"
    type: integration
    component_id: poc/main.js-refactor
    description: "main.js calls builtinClickProvider.init(ctx) before metronome.start() is enabled"
    inputs: |
      Simulate Start button click
      Mock builtinClickProvider.init() to resolve after a delay
    expected_output: |
      builtinClickProvider.init(ctx) is called with the newly created AudioContext
      Metro Play button is disabled until init() resolves
      createMetronome is called AFTER init() resolves
      Metro Play button is enabled and metronome is ready to start
    determinism_note: "Mocked async operations"

  - id: "tc-042"
    type: integration
    component_id: poc/main.js-refactor
    description: "main.js registers ContentProviders and SampleProviders in correct sequence"
    inputs: |
      Simulate page load through Start button
      Monitor registry registration calls
    expected_output: |
      contentService.register(localFileProvider) is called
      contentService.register(recordingsProvider) is called
      registry.register(builtinClickProvider) is called (at import time)
      SampleSetPicker can create MediaPoolSampleProvider and register it
    determinism_note: "Deterministic call sequence"

  - id: "tc-043"
    type: integration
    component_id: poc/main.js-refactor
    description: "main.js wires Export Workspace and Copy Workspace buttons"
    inputs: |
      Find export-workspace-btn and copy-workspace-btn elements
      Click Export Workspace button
      Mock file download
    expected_output: |
      downloadWorkspace(components) is called
      File is downloaded as 'workspace.yaml'
      Copy Workspace button calls copyWorkspace(components)
      YAML is written to clipboard
    determinism_note: "Mocked file download and clipboard"

  - id: "tc-044"
    type: integration
    component_id: poc/main.js-refactor
    description: "main.js calls registerDropTarget(components) for workspace drop import"
    inputs: |
      Drop a valid workspace.yaml file on the document
    expected_output: |
      importWorkspace is called with file contents and name
      On Apply, all sections are updated
      Drop target persists across multiple file drops
    determinism_note: "Mocked file drop events"

coverage_gaps:
  - requirement: "index-html-global-toolbar (lane-c): DOM structure and button ids"
    rationale: "HTML-only change; verified by visual inspection and browser test"
  - requirement: "index-html-file-import-ui (lane-d): file input and button element IDs"
    rationale: "HTML-only change; verified by visual inspection and browser test"
  - requirement: "index-html-preset-bank-dom (lane-f): preset bank container and save button"
    rationale: "HTML-only change; verified by visual inspection and browser test"
  - requirement: "index-html-alignment-monitor-dom (lane-g): canvas element and DOM ordering"
    rationale: "HTML-only change; verified by visual inspection and browser test"
  - requirement: "poc/index-html-final-assembly (lane-wire): full HTML assembly and script ordering"
    rationale: "HTML-only change; integration verified by running the POC in a browser"
  - requirement: "Workspace file download via URL.createObjectURL (config/workspace)"
    rationale: "Browser API integration; requires browser environment; tested in browser acceptance suite"
  - requirement: "Clipboard API usage (navigator.clipboard.writeText)"
    rationale: "Requires secure context (HTTPS or localhost); tested in browser environment"
  - requirement: "File Picker API (showOpenFilePicker) fallback behavior"
    rationale: "Browser API; FSAA behavior requires actual user interaction; browser test only"
  - requirement: "AudioContext and Web Audio API integration"
    rationale: "Cross-module Web Audio graph assembly tested via mocked OfflineAudioContext"
  - requirement: "Drag-and-drop file import and decodeAudioData"
    rationale: "Requires browser drop event and actual audio decoding; browser acceptance test"

verdict: ready-for-review
```
